response:
# Triage plan ‚Äî top 10 by aggregate risk

Ranked by severity then `agent_risk` (the PRD's default ordering, since `agent_risk` is what makes a `crimes` finding worth surfacing over a linter result).

## Structural

| # | ID | Charge | Where | Why it ranks | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | crime_00001 | God Function | `src/billing.ts:50‚Äì253` (`generateInvoice`) | Only **high** in the report; 204 lines, agent_risk 0.63. Edits here are the single biggest "agent misses an interaction" risk in the repo. | **Fix now** ‚Äî extract cohesive sections before any further billing change lands. |
| 3 | crime_00005 | Temporal Recklessness | `src/date.ts:4‚Äì22` | 5 direct clock reads in a helper module that everything else imports ‚Äî `test_gap` 1.0. Cheap to fix and unblocks deterministic tests everywhere. | **Fix now** ‚Äî inject a `now()` here first; ripples to crime_00004 for free. |
| 9 | crime_00004 | Temporal Recklessness | `src/billing.ts` (7 occurrences) | Same root cause as 00005 but inside the God Function. Cleanest if done as part of the 00001 extraction. | **Defer** ‚Äî bundle into the crime_00001 fix, not a separate change. |
| 10 | crime_00008 | False Identity | `src/billing.ts:40‚Äì44` (`calculateRefundPreview`) | "preview" name but calls `saveRefundAudit` + `sendRefundEmail`. Agents will treat it as pure and double-fire side effects. | **Fix now** ‚Äî rename/split before refactoring the surrounding billing code. |
| ‚Äî | crime_00013 | Unfinished Business | `src/todo.ts` (14 markers, 424/kLOC) | High density but the file is named `todo.ts` ‚Äî likely a scratchpad, not load-bearing. | **Defer** ‚Äî triage in a sweep; not blocking. |

## IA (information architecture)

| # | ID | Charge | Where | Why it ranks | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | crime_00002 | Missing Agent Context | repo root | No `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` / `SKILL.md`; `package.json` declares a `messy` bin agents can't discover. Cheapest highest-leverage fix in the report. | **Fix now** ‚Äî add `AGENTS.md` first; everything downstream becomes easier for the next agent that touches this. |
| 5 | crime_00009 | Concept Alias Drift | docs + `src/routes/{account,team,workspace}` | 4 aliases for "tenant" across 5 files. Will cause an agent to add a 5th vocabulary instead of extending one. | **Defer** ‚Äî needs a product decision on canonical term before code moves. |

## Frontend / routing

| # | ID | Charge | Where | Why it ranks | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | crime_00012 | Duplicated Navigation Source | `src/nav/registry.ts` + `src/nav/sidebar.ts` | Same destination `/settings/billing`, two labels ("Plans" vs "Billing"). Any agent fixing one label leaves the other stale. | **Fix now** ‚Äî pick one source of truth; small, mechanical. |
| 8 | crime_00014 | Route Metadata Drift | `src/routes/settings/billing.tsx` | Same destination labelled 4 ways across path/file/component/title/nav. Bundles naturally with crime_00012. | **Fix now** ‚Äî same PR as 00012; choose canonical name once. |

## Duplication

| # | ID | Charge | Where | Why it ranks | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | crime_00007 | String Sprinkles | `"enterprise"` √ó 4 files (`api`, `billing`, `jobs`, `ui`) | Domain literal scattered across all 4 layers. Adding the 5th copy is how a plan-tier policy bug ships. | **Fix now** ‚Äî centralise into a plan/tier constant or enum before any plan-tier work. |

## Git-history

None of the top 10. Every finding has `churn: 0` ‚Äî the fixture has no git history, so `hotspots` would currently say nothing. Worth noting in the verdict report rather than triaging.

---

## Suggested execution order

1. **crime_00002** (AGENTS.md) ‚Äî unblocks everything else for agents, ~15 min.
2. **crime_00007** (`"enterprise"` ‚Üí constant) ‚Äî prevents new copies during subsequent edits.
3. **crime_00012 + crime_00014** (nav + route metadata) ‚Äî one PR, mechanical.
4. **crime_00008** (rename `calculateRefundPreview`) ‚Äî make billing safer to touch.
5. **crime_00005** (clock injection in `date.ts`) ‚Äî enables deterministic tests.
6. **crime_00001 + crime_00004** (split `generateInvoice`, inject clock as you go) ‚Äî the big one; do it with the safety nets above in place.
7. Defer: **crime_00009** (alias drift ‚Äî needs naming decision), **crime_00013** (TODO sweep).

No findings in the top 10 warrant **ignore** ‚Äî all are medium+ with concrete evidence. The 12 remaining low-severity findings (orphaned routes, weak-assertion tests, option bags) can roll into a later cleanup pass.
